feed.title: Pupilla
feed.subtitle: A multidisciplinary preprint archive with the aim of building bridges and striving for unity in diversity.

entry.title: Being with Suffering
entry.content: In this article, a psychotherapeutic counsellor reflects on how to be present with suffering, drawing on the life of Christ and insights from psychotherapy. Exploring Jesus Crucified and Forsaken as God's identification with human pain, and Chiara Lubich's bridal mysticism of encountering Jesus Forsaken within every suffering, the author connects these spiritual insights with psychological concepts from Gestalt therapy, Acceptance and Commitment Therapy, and Logotherapy.
entry.summary: In this article, a psychotherapeutic counsellor reflects on how to be present with suffering, drawing on the life of Christ and insights from psychotherapy. Exploring Jesus Crucified and Forsaken as God's identification with human pain, and Chiara Lubich's bridal mysticism of encountering Jesus Forsaken within every suffering, the author connects these spiritual insights with psychological concepts from Gestalt therapy, Acceptance and Commitment Therapy, and Logotherapy.

entry.title: Science in Europe Between War and Peace

link.title: Science in Europe Between War and Peace

entry.content: This article examines the paradoxical relationship between science and society in contemporary Europe, where public trust in science has reached unprecedented levels even as misinformation and political polarization increasingly challenge evidence-based policymaking. Against the backdrop of the COVID-19 pandemic, climate change, and artificial intelligence, the author explores what he terms the "technocratic dilemma" — the tension between expert knowledge and democratic decision-making.
entry.summary: This article examines the paradoxical relationship between science and society in contemporary Europe, where public trust in science has reached unprecedented levels even as misinformation and political polarization increasingly challenge evidence-based policymaking. Against the backdrop of the COVID-19 pandemic, climate change, and artificial intelligence, the author explores what he terms the "technocratic dilemma" — the tension between expert knowledge and democratic decision-making.

entry.title: L'ateismo Cristiano di Slavoj Žižek: un dialogo con Chiara Lubich

link.title: L'ateismo Cristiano di Slavoj Žižek: un dialogo con Chiara Lubich

entry.content: This essay examines the thought of Slovenian philosopher Slavoj Žižek on Christianity, with particular attention to his interpretation of God's death on the cross and the birth of the Holy Spirit as an 'egalitarian community.' Through a comparative analysis with Chiara Lubich's charism of unity, the article highlights surprising convergences between Žižek's 'Christian atheism' and Focolare spirituality.
entry.summary: This essay examines the thought of Slovenian philosopher Slavoj Žižek on Christianity, with particular attention to his interpretation of God's death on the cross and the birth of the Holy Spirit as an 'egalitarian community.' Through a comparative analysis with Chiara Lubich's charism of unity, the article highlights surprising convergences between Žižek's 'Christian atheism' and Focolare spirituality.

entry.title: Tra realtà e immaginazione. Brevi note e riflessioni a partire dall’evoluzione del concetto di spazio in matematica.

link.title: Tra realtà e immaginazione. Brevi note e riflessioni a partire dall’evoluzione del concetto di spazio in matematica.

entry.content: This essay traces the evolution of the concept of space in mathematics from ancient Greece to modern times, examining the philosophical and cultural implications of this transformation. Beginning with Euclidean geometry as an absolute representation of physical reality, the authors explore how Cartesian coordinate systems introduced a subjective perspective that challenged the notion of absolute space.
entry.summary: This essay traces the evolution of the concept of space in mathematics from ancient Greece to modern times, examining the philosophical and cultural implications of this transformation. Beginning with Euclidean geometry as an absolute representation of physical reality, the authors explore how Cartesian coordinate systems introduced a subjective perspective that challenged the notion of absolute space.

entry.title: Pluralismo y Evangelio: dialogar para promover una cultura del encuentro

link.title: Pluralismo y Evangelio: dialogar para promover una cultura del encuentro

entry.content: This article examines the relationship between Gospel and cultures within the context of pluralism as a defining characteristic of the current epochal change, explaining the urgency of evangelization as a culture of encounter in service of unity in diversity. Drawing primarily on Pope Francis's magisterium, particularly his Apostolic Exhortation Evangelii Gaudium (2013), and updating Chiara Lubich's intuition about inculturation as an exchange of gifts, the study addresses how the Church must...
entry.summary: This article examines the relationship between Gospel and cultures within the context of pluralism as a defining characteristic of the current epochal change, explaining the urgency of evangelization as a culture of encounter in service of unity in diversity. Drawing primarily on Pope Francis's magisterium, particularly his Apostolic Exhortation Evangelii Gaudium (2013), and updating Chiara Lubich's intuition about inculturation as an exchange of gifts, the study addresses how the Church must continuously relocate itself within diverse local and global contexts.

entry.title: El diálogo intercultural (e interreligioso) como experiencia mística del nosotros

link.title: El diálogo intercultural (e interreligioso) como experiencia mística del nosotros

entry.content: This article examines the possibility of experiencing intercultural dialogue as "dialogical dialogue" (R. Panikkar) that generates a "mystical experience of we" (Pope Francis) within the context of contemporary pluralism. The author argues that the current epoch change evidences the emergence of a pluralist stage that challenges Christian faith to give renewed and creative reasons for belief.
entry.summary: This article examines the possibility of experiencing intercultural dialogue as "dialogical dialogue" (R. Panikkar) that generates a "mystical experience of we" (Pope Francis) within the context of contemporary pluralism. The author argues that the current epoch change evidences the emergence of a pluralist stage that challenges Christian faith to give renewed and creative reasons for belief.

entry.title: Intuizioni e chiavi per una possibile missione interculturale

link.title: Intuizioni e chiavi per una possibile missione interculturale

entry.content: This article explores Pope Francis's teaching that "in the heart of God there is a preferential place for the poor" (Evangelii Gaudium 197), examining the theological and pastoral implications of recognizing the poor as a privileged "theological locus." Through personal testimony from the author's experience with Juan Carlos, a street child in Cochabamba, Bolivia, the text illustrates how encounters with the poor can become living encounters with Christ himself.
entry.summary: This article explores Pope Francis's teaching that "in the heart of God there is a preferential place for the poor" (Evangelii Gaudium 197), examining the theological and pastoral implications of recognizing the poor as a privileged "theological locus." Through personal testimony from the author's experience with Juan Carlos, a street child in Cochabamba, Bolivia, the text illustrates how encounters with the poor can become living encounters with Christ himself.

entry.title: Nel seno della Trinità c’è un posto privilegiato per i poveri: I poveri come «luogo teologico»

link.title: Nel seno della Trinità c’è un posto privilegiato per i poveri: I poveri come «luogo teologico»

entry.content: This article explores Pope Francis's teaching that "in the heart of God there is a preferential place for the poor" (Evangelii Gaudium 197), examining the theological and pastoral implications of recognizing the poor as a privileged "theological locus." Through personal testimony from the author's experience with Juan Carlos, a street child in Cochabamba, Bolivia, the text illustrates how encounters with the poor can become living encounters with Christ himself.
entry.summary: This article explores Pope Francis's teaching that "in the heart of God there is a preferential place for the poor" (Evangelii Gaudium 197), examining the theological and pastoral implications of recognizing the poor as a privileged "theological locus." Through personal testimony from the author's experience with Juan Carlos, a street child in Cochabamba, Bolivia, the text illustrates how encounters with the poor can become living encounters with Christ himself.

entry.title: Lectura intercultural/pluralista del Pacto de unidad como experiencia sapiencial

link.title: Lectura intercultural/pluralista del Pacto de unidad como experiencia sapiencial

entry.content: This paper explores the possibility of actualizing the Pact of Unity between Chiara Lubich and Igino Giornani (July 16, 1949) within contemporary pluralistic contexts. The author investigates whether this foundational mystical experience of the Focolare Movement can transcend its Catholic confessional framework to embrace intercultural and interreligious dialogue, including non-confessional spiritual approaches.
entry.summary: This paper explores the possibility of actualizing the Pact of Unity between Chiara Lubich and Igino Giornani (July 16, 1949) within contemporary pluralistic contexts. The author investigates whether this foundational mystical experience of the Focolare Movement can transcend its Catholic confessional framework to embrace intercultural and interreligious dialogue, including non-confessional spiritual approaches.

entry.title: El Abandonado de Chiara Lubich
entry.content: This study presents a triple interpretative framework for understanding Chiara Lubich's (1920-2008) texts on Jesus Forsaken through theological, mystical, and philosophical perspectives. The central focus is Lubich's interpretation of the crucified Jesus crying out 'My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?' (Mark 15:34), which she positions as the cornerstone of a spirituality aimed at achieving universal fraternity.
entry.summary: This study presents a triple interpretative framework for understanding Chiara Lubich's (1920-2008) texts on Jesus Forsaken through theological, mystical, and philosophical perspectives. The central focus is Lubich's interpretation of the crucified Jesus crying out 'My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?' (Mark 15:34), which she positions as the cornerstone of a spirituality aimed at achieving universal fraternity.

entry.title: Who are Chiara Lubich's «two or three»?

link.title: Who are Chiara Lubich's «two or three»?

entry.content: This paper examines Chiara Lubich's radical reinterpretation of Jesus' promise in Matthew 18:20 about two or three gathered in his name. Drawing from Lubich's mystical insights during the Paradise of '49 and her extensive talks spanning several decades, the study analyzes her revolutionary understanding of who can experience Christ's presence and under what conditions.
entry.summary: This paper examines Chiara Lubich's radical reinterpretation of Jesus' promise in Matthew 18:20 about two or three gathered in his name. Drawing from Lubich's mystical insights during the Paradise of '49 and her extensive talks spanning several decades, the study analyzes her revolutionary understanding of who can experience Christ's presence and under what conditions.
